summary: Independent read-only QA does not accept M7-TUTORIAL-HINTS-ENCYCLOPEDIA-COMPLETE-001. Scope and focused test reproduction are clean, but the M7 tutorial/hints/encyclopedia read model embeds a local M7 content-record-shaped pack that drifts from the accepted M7 scenario/person/event content pack. Player-facing encyclopedia labels and review states can therefore disagree with accepted content review tasks, so the implementation fails the requirement that historical/cultural terms shown in tutorial, hints, and encyclopedia preserve accepted labels and review status.

tests_run.result: Read required project status. It records current M7 context, M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS, M7 content-lock boundaries, and DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE preservation.
On feat/m7-tutorial-hints-encyclopedia. Before this QA handoff correction, the only untracked file was the known unrelated docs/M7_UI_Localization_Art_Handoff_Prompts.md.

tests_run.command: git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD; git rev-parse HEAD; git merge-base origin/main HEAD
tests_run.result: Branch feat/m7-tutorial-hints-encyclopedia; actual reviewed HEAD 489cabb3bb064baf37ae1d1723a2a0233c2d0c60; merge-base 71fcb49f2cf7aeb0022eb7d72792c4aca8c4ada7.
Changed paths are limited to allowed web, client-core, ui, tests, Storybook, task, message, route, goal-mode, model-routing, and thread-registry paths.

tests_run.command: git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD -- forbidden path set
tests_run.result: No forbidden-path diff for pnpm-lock.yaml, package manifests, apps/desktop, apps/sim-runner, apps/content-tools, packages/sim-core, packages/sim-ai, packages/protocol, packages/save-format, packages/map-renderer, packages/platform, tools, CI, prior gates, roadmap, risk/acceptance docs, PROJECT_STATUS.md, MANIFEST.md, SHA256SUMS.txt, or VALIDATION_REPORT.md.

tests_run.result: No package manifest or lockfile dependency drift.
Ready tasks are M7-AUDIO-ART-LOCALIZATION-COMPLETE-001 and M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001; this REVIEW task is not listed as ready.
No forbidden or unexpected sim/content-runtime imports were found in the reviewed client surfaces.
No WorldState storage, real-time/random APIs, Node fs/electron imports, or DOM document access were found in the reviewed client read-model/UI surfaces.

tests_run.command: node -e accepted M7 beta content pack summary and label comparison
tests_run.result: Accepted content-source/m7-beta-scenarios/beta-scenario-person-event-set.json has 3 scenarios, 6 persons, 3 titles, 5 events, 36 localization records, 6 claimRecords, and review labels such as HIST-M7-FILL-001-SCOPE label INFERRED and content.m7.beta.person.court_broker_1531.name reviewState LANGUAGE_REVIEW_REQUIRED.

decisions.reason: The M7 guidance UI/read-model path does not reliably preserve accepted M7 content labels and review states because it embeds a local compact content pack that has drifted from the accepted M7 content-source pack.
No forbidden paths, package dependency drift, lockfile drift, desktop/sim/protocol/save/map/platform/tool/CI/PROJECT_STATUS/prior gate/roadmap drift were found in origin/main...HEAD.

The UI consumes a ClientM7GuidanceReadModelSnapshot and does not import sim-core or hold WorldState, but the read model duplicates accepted M7 content records instead of using or checking against the accepted content pack.
No telemetry, remote collection, account, cloud, server, multiplayer, arbitrary-code mod, release, branding, or manual node battle content was introduced; formal content lock remains not accepted.
The added tests assert the stale local fixture values and would not fail when labels/review states drift from content-source/m7-beta-scenarios/beta-scenario-person-event-set.json.

risks.summary: Player-facing M7 tutorial and encyclopedia labels can present stale or wrong review status compared with accepted M7 content review tasks.
risks.impact: Historical/cultural uncertainty and language-review gates can be weakened in the UI even though accepted content-source data still records them correctly.

risks.summary: The Storybook and Playwright state coverage is structurally useful, but currently proves rendering of the local pack rather than conformance with accepted content records.

blockers.reproduction: Compare project/tasks/active/M7-SCENARIO-PERSON-EVENT-FILL-001.json lines 197-203 and content-source/m7-beta-scenarios/beta-scenario-person-event-set.json lines 54-57, 149-152, 331-339, 461-469 against packages/client-core/src/index.ts lines 1789-1796, 1819-1823, 1913-1921, 2019-2028, and 4863-4909.
blockers.observed: The accepted content pack records 3 scenarios, 6 persons, 3 titles, 5 events, and 36 localization records. The UI-local pack records only 3 scenarios, 1 person, 1 title, 1 event, and 12 localization records. Accepted claim HIST-M7-FILL-001-SCOPE is label INFERRED with confidence HIGH, but the UI-local pack redefines it as FICTIONAL. Accepted person key content.m7.beta.person.court_broker_1531.name is LANGUAGE_REVIEW_REQUIRED, but the UI-local pack marks the same key SCHEMA_VALIDATED. Accepted event key content.m7.beta.event.coercion_cost_review.name displays Forced movement and population-loss review, but the UI-local pack displays Coercion cost review.
blockers.impact: This fails the acceptance criterion that historical/cultural terms shown in tutorial, hints, or encyclopedia use the accepted labels and review status from content review tasks. Because packages/ui/src/index.tsx renders entry.contentLabel, entry.confidence, and entry.reviewState directly, stale local data reaches player-facing encyclopedia surfaces.
blockers.necessary_fix: Drive the M7 guidance read model from the accepted M7 content-source/runtime pack, or add a deterministic generated/client fixture that is mechanically checked against content-source/m7-beta-scenarios/beta-scenario-person-event-set.json. Update tests so mismatched labels, reviewState values, localization strings, known-gap counts, or contracted counts fail.

requested_action: Repair the M7 guidance read model so tutorial, hint, and encyclopedia surfaces preserve accepted M7 content labels/review states and add discriminating tests against the accepted content pack; then return a new writer REVIEW handoff for qa_reviewer. Lead orchestrator should coordinate the repair route.
